text_en:
A large law firm is building an AI solution to automatically extract important legal information from court rulings and contracts, and generate summary reports. The solution needs to identify key legal entities (such as parties, rulings, and clauses) from free-form legal documents and convert them into organized summaries.

Which solution BEST meets these requirements?
explanation_en:
Amazon Comprehend Medical is an NLP service specialized in domain-specific structured entity extraction and relationship analysis. Its core capability is identifying domain-specific entities and relationships from unstructured text and converting them into organized formats.

• Amazon Comprehend Medical: An NLP service specialized in extracting domain-specific structured information, capable of identifying legal entities and relationships from unstructured text.
• Amazon Personalize: A recommendation engine based on user behavior, not suitable for this scenario requiring legal entity extraction and summary generation.
• Amazon Textract: A document image-to-text conversion tool that lacks the capability to analyze legal entities and relationships in extracted text.
• Amazon Kendra: A service specialized in search and search result ranking, without automatic entity extraction and structured information analysis capabilities.
key_points_en:
Domain-Specific NLP Entity Extraction
• Amazon Comprehend Medical extracts structured entities and relationships from domain-specific unstructured text
• Summary reports can be generated by applying rule-based logic to extraction results
• Amazon Textract (image-to-text) and Comprehend (text-to-insights) have different roles

options.text_en: Amazon Comprehend Medical
options.explanation_en: An NLP service specialized in extracting domain-specific structured information, capable of identifying legal entities and relationships from unstructured text and converting them into summaries.

options.text_en: Amazon Personalize
options.explanation_en: A recommendation engine based on user behavior, not suitable for this scenario requiring legal entity extraction and summary generation.

options.text_en: Amazon Textract
options.explanation_en: A tool that converts scanned documents to text. It lacks the NLP capability to analyze legal entities and relationships in extracted text.

options.text_en: Amazon Kendra
options.explanation_en: A service specialized in building search indexes, lacking automatic entity extraction and structured information analysis capabilities to meet these requirements.

text_en:
An e-commerce company is building a recommendation system. The company needs to convert each product and customer behavior into low-dimensional numerical vectors so that the computer can understand and compare customer purchase patterns and product characteristics.

What is this technique called, and what are its characteristics?
explanation_en:
Embedding is a technique that converts high-dimensional complex information like words, images, and product data into low-dimensional numerical vector space. These converted vectors preserve semantic similarity, so in a recommendation system, the similarity between two products can be obtained through simple vector distance calculations.

• Data Compression: A technique for compressing large data to reduce storage space, different from embedding's purpose.
• Data Encryption: A technique for transforming data for security, unrelated to embedding.
• High-Dimensional Data Visualization: A technique for visualizing data in 2D/3D graphs (such as t-SNE), not the primary purpose of embedding.
• Low-Dimensional Numerical Vector Transformation: The precise definition of embedding, where converted vectors preserve the semantic relationships of original data.
key_points_en:
Embedding
• A technique that converts high-dimensional data into low-dimensional numerical vector space
• Vector distances preserve the semantic similarity of original data
• Core foundation for recommendation systems, search, NLP, and generative AI models

options.text_en: A technique to reduce storage space through data compression
options.explanation_en: This is the definition of data compression. Embedding is not primarily aimed at reducing storage space, but rather at representing data's semantic relationships as numerical vectors.

options.text_en: A method to enhance security by encrypting sensitive data
options.explanation_en: This describes encryption. Embedding does not serve data security purposes.

options.text_en: A method to visualize high-dimensional data as 2D or 3D graphs
options.explanation_en: This describes dimensionality reduction visualization techniques (t-SNE, PCA, etc.). Embedding itself is not aimed at visualization.

options.text_en: A method to convert high-dimensional data into low-dimensional numerical vector space
options.explanation_en: The precise definition of embedding. The converted vectors preserve the semantic relationships of original data, serving as a core foundation for recommendation systems, NLP, and generative AI models.

text_en:
A global financial company is building an AI application to analyze complex regulatory reports and customer transaction records. The application must process thousands of regulatory documents and transaction histories of varying lengths each month, but some documents fail to be analyzed.

Why does the application fail to analyze some documents?
explanation_en:
LLM models have a limitation called context window, which is the maximum size of input tokens they can process. When very long regulatory documents or transaction records exceed the maximum input token count that the model can handle, analysis fails.

• Temperature Parameter: A parameter controlling the creativity and diversity of responses, unrelated to document analysis failure.
• Fine-tuning Support: An optional choice for domain-specific learning, not the direct cause of analysis failure.
• Top P Value: A parameter controlling the diversity of token sampling, not the cause of analysis failure itself.
• Input Tokens Exceed Limit: When LLM processing exceeds the maximum context size it can handle, analysis fails. This can be resolved by switching to a model with larger context window or by chunking documents for processing.
key_points_en:
Context Window
• A limitation on the maximum number of input tokens an LLM can process
• When input exceeds context size, processing failure occurs
• Can be resolved through document chunking or selecting a model with larger context window

options.text_en: The temperature parameter is set too high
options.explanation_en: Temperature is a parameter that controls the creativity level of responses. A higher value generates more diverse answers but does not cause analysis failure itself.

options.text_en: The selected model does not support fine-tuning
options.explanation_en: Fine-tuning is an optional choice to adapt the model to a specific domain, not the direct cause of analysis failure.

options.text_en: The Top P value is set too high
options.explanation_en: Top P is a parameter controlling the diversity of token sampling, not the direct cause of analysis failure phenomenon.

options.text_en: Input tokens exceed the model's context window size
options.explanation_en: LLM models have a fixed maximum input token size (context window) they can process. When very long regulatory documents or large amounts of transaction records exceed this limit, analysis fails.

text_en:
An e-commerce company is building an AI assistant for its customer service team that can automatically answer questions about product information, shipping status, and return policies. The company wants to use Large Language Models (LLMs) and internal knowledge bases to create a text-based chatbot interface.

What solution can meet these requirements while minimizing development effort?
explanation_en:
A Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) agent using Amazon Bedrock meets the requirements while minimizing development effort. Amazon Bedrock is a managed service that provides foundational LLMs, and the RAG pattern for integrating with enterprise knowledge bases can be easily implemented.

• Amazon SageMaker Autopilot: Focuses on automated machine learning training, requiring additional development.
• Amazon Bedrock: A managed LLM service that can efficiently integrate with knowledge bases through RAG, enabling chatbot development with minimal development effort.
• Amazon Q Developer: A code assistance tool for developers, not suitable for customer service chatbot scenarios.
• Amazon SageMaker JumpStart: A fine-tuning service based on pre-trained models, but requires model training process, increasing development effort.
key_points_en:
Implementing RAG with Amazon Bedrock
• A managed LLM service providing pre-trained Foundation Models
• Integration with enterprise knowledge bases for accurate response generation
• Fast deployment without separate model training

options.text_en: Amazon SageMaker Autopilot
options.explanation_en: An automated ML training tool, but requires additional development and data preparation work for chatbot building, requiring more development effort.

options.text_en: Amazon Bedrock
options.explanation_en: Can efficiently combine enterprise knowledge bases with Foundation Models through RAG to build chatbot with minimal development effort.

options.text_en: Amazon Q Developer
options.explanation_en: A code assistance tool for developers, with different purpose from customer service chatbots or product information systems.

options.text_en: Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
options.explanation_en: A service for fine-tuning based on pre-trained models, but requires model training and adjustment process, requiring more development effort.

text_en:
An AI team at a financial company is building a sentiment analysis model based on customer feedback data. Before inputting customer review text (for example: "This service is really great and fast!") to the model, a preprocessing step is needed. Developers have decided to split this text into smaller units such as "This", "service", "is", "really", "great", and "fast".

What is the purpose of this preprocessing technique?
explanation_en:
Tokenization is a core technique in natural language processing that splits text into meaningful smaller units (tokens). This allows the model to understand the structure of text and recognize patterns.

• Text Encryption: Not the purpose of tokenization; separate encryption techniques are required for security.
• Text File Compression: Unrelated to compression algorithms that reduce file size.
• Splitting text into smaller units for processing: The precise definition of tokenization.
• Translation of text between languages: Tokenization is splitting within a single language, translation is a separate task.
key_points_en:
Tokenization
• A core preprocessing technique in Natural Language Processing (NLP) that splits text into meaningful tokens
• Various tokenization approaches exist at word, character, and subword levels
• Used as the input preprocessing stage for all NLP models including sentiment analysis and text classification

options.text_en: To encrypt text data
options.explanation_en: Tokenization is not an encryption technique for data security. It is a preprocessing technique that splits text into meaningful units.

options.text_en: To compress text files
options.explanation_en: The purpose of tokenization is not to reduce file size through compression, but to split text into smaller units that the model can understand.

options.text_en: To split text into smaller units for processing
options.explanation_en: The precise definition of tokenization. When splitting words or sentences into tokens, the model can learn the meaning and relationships of each unit individually.

options.text_en: To translate text into another language
options.explanation_en: Tokenization is text splitting within a single language, and translation between languages is a separate task.
